problem_statement.fact: The remaining branch row has a structurally strong p90_k100_d5 pool: 100 finite candidates, 100 reachable/standoff candidates, 5 positive-support candidates, no duplicate candidate ids, and no nonfinite positions.
problem_statement.agent_inference: A simple action-time pool-validity proxy based on candidate count, reachability, duplicate rate, positive support, or score shape would incorrectly treat this row as pool-valid, because the recovered rows are not stronger on those features.
problem_statement.paper_constraint: This is a diagnostic backend branch chosen after evaluation-only no-valid reporting. It cannot be used as a paper-facing action policy unless the fallback rule is applied to a predeclared action-time source or all backend-branch rows without choosing rows from labels.

rejected_proxy_families.name: candidate_count_threshold
rejected_proxy_families.reason: The no-valid branch has 100 candidates, matching the recovered rows.

rejected_proxy_families.name: reachability_or_standoff_threshold
rejected_proxy_families.reason: The no-valid branch has 100 reachable/standoff candidates, while recovered rows have 81.

rejected_proxy_families.name: positive_support_count_threshold
rejected_proxy_families.reason: The no-valid branch and recovered rows all have 5 positive-support candidates after deeper generation.

rejected_proxy_families.name: score_shape_threshold
rejected_proxy_families.reason: The no-valid branch has high top/rank20/rank100 scores and is not separated from recovered rows by semantic score magnitude.

rejected_proxy_families.name: duplicate_or_nonfinite_guard
rejected_proxy_families.reason: The no-valid branch has zero duplicate candidate ids and zero nonfinite candidate positions.

proxy_assessment.analysis_only_note: The no-valid label is used only to evaluate proxy separability and choose the diagnostic branch contract. It is forbidden as an action-time input to the later analyzer/job.

first_fallback_variant.reason: Lower the percentile and reduce spatial NMS distance to widen the same non-GT VLMaps backend before switching backend family.

second_fallback_variant_if_first_fallbac.reason: Use component-level export only if the fixed spatial NMS fallback remains structurally valid but no-valid after evaluation-only join.

allowed_action_inputs: backend/pool-validity branch rows written before label join
deeper generation action rows and candidate pool accounting from spatial_nms_p90_k100_d5_v1
candidate ids, ranks, scores, semantic scores, support scores, positive support, positions, visit positions, reachability, finite-position metadata, duplicate accounting, and backend lineage
existing non-GT VLMaps feature maps, text embeddings, alignment, spatial NMS or component export settings
Habitat navmesh and reachability or standoff feasibility computed without GT target labels
forbidden_action_inputs: choosing fallback threshold, top-k, NMS distance, or backend family after inspecting fallback candidate correctness
terminal commit or goal switch based on this branch contract

proxy_rejection_accounting: report why action-time candidate count, reachability, duplicate/nonfinite, positive-support, and score-shape proxies cannot isolate the no-valid row
report recovered-row reference statistics only in analysis/evaluation summary, not action rows
fallback_generation_accounting: report fallback backend variant name, percentile, max candidates, NMS distance or component settings
report retained-from-previous-pool, new candidate count, duplicate candidate ids, duplicate positions, finite-position count, reachable/standoff count, and positive-support count
report target scene/query artifact rows and missing artifact cases
evaluation_only_reporting: write fallback generation rows before joining labels
join evaluation labels only in evaluated rows or summary
report whether the fallback variant recovers a valid candidate pool after label join
do not change fallback variant based on the same fallback label join

expected_next_analyzer_or_job.name: expanded_retrieval_pool_validity_branch_v1
expected_next_analyzer_or_job.role: consume the single backend/pool-validity branch row, reject unsafe structural proxies, and materialize a fixed fallback backend variant before any goal-validity confirmation
expected_next_analyzer_or_job.input_scope: backend_pool_validity_branch_rows.jsonl from h001_expanded_retrieval_goal_validity_confirmation_v1 and label-free deeper generation rows

promotion_rules.contract_only: Freezing this contract permits a diagnostic fallback backend analyzer/job, not terminal ObjectNav commitment.
promotion_rules.if_fallback_recovers_valid_pool: The row may move to a later goal-validity confirmation evidence branch only after the fallback rows are written label-free and recovery is checked by evaluation-only join.
promotion_rules.if_fallback_still_no_valid: Mark bxsVRursffK::bed as a backend/source-map recall blind spot and keep terminal utility blocked for this branch.
promotion_rules.paper_claim_blocked: No first_eval rerun, policy-scale comparison, or paper utility claim is allowed from this branch contract alone.

next_steps: Implement a small branch analyzer that records proxy rejection and loads or generates the fixed spatial_nms_p80_k200_d3_v1 fallback artifact for bxsVRursffK::bed.
If the fallback artifact does not exist, launch the generation job in tmux/nohup with timestamped logs under the H001 runtime logs directory.
Join labels only after fallback generation rows are written and report whether the pool becomes eligible for later goal-validity confirmation.
Keep candidate-specific goal-validity evidence for rival_identity:12 and rival_identity:14 as a separate branch.
